research_question: Can the anti-J-edge bounce-LONG watcher family be RESCUED by (A) a regime gate that suppresses longs in catching-a-falling-knife conditions (BEAR ribbon + VIX rising + below VWAP), or (B) a short-side inversion that fires WITH J's bearish-continuation edge? Or do we RETIRE the family? Real-fills (OPRA) is the only authority (C1).

hypotheses.A_regime_gate: Suppress bounce-LONG when ribbon stack==BEAR AND vix_now>vix_prior AND close<session_VWAP. PROMOTE iff this flips edge_capture POSITIVE and real-fills exp>0 and DSR!=FAIL.
hypotheses.B_short_inversion: Isolate the SHORT signals (SECOND_TEST lower-high resistance leg + all of CLOSE_CEILING_FADE). PROMOTE iff shorts show edge_capture POSITIVE (fire WITH J on down days) and real-fills exp>0 and DSR!=FAIL.

gate_definition: PROMOTE-CANDIDATE iff real-fills exp>0 AND anchor edge_capture>0 (fires WITH J, not against) AND DSR/PBO advisory verdict != FAIL. If neither hypothesis rescues a pattern → RETIRE-WITH-EVIDENCE (playbook: fail thresholds → retire, not loosen; OP-22). Honest no-win is a valid result — do NOT manufacture a win.

FLOOR_HOLD_BOUNCE.pattern_verdict: RETIRE-WITH-EVIDENCE — neither hypothesis flips this pattern to real-fills-positive AND edge_capture-positive AND DSR!=FAIL. baseline-long real-fills exp=$-19.67 (n=111), edge_capture=$-150.0 [NEGATIVE]; Hyp A regime-gate: exp=$-21.3 (n=102, suppressed 10 hostile-regime longs), edge_capture=$-150.0 [NEGATIVE]; Hyp B short-inversion: no short mirror exists in this detector (long-only). Per playbook 'setups that fail thresholds get retired, not loosened' + OP-22, this is a clean honest no-win.

CLOSE_CEILING_FADE.pattern_verdict: RETIRE-WITH-EVIDENCE — neither hypothesis flips this pattern to real-fills-positive AND edge_capture-positive AND DSR!=FAIL. baseline-long real-fills exp=$-40.59 (n=141), edge_capture=$-69.0 [NEGATIVE]; Hyp A regime-gate: n/a (no long leg); Hyp B short-inversion: exp=$-40.59 (n=141), edge_capture=$-69.0 [NEGATIVE]. Per playbook 'setups that fail thresholds get retired, not loosened' + OP-22, this is a clean honest no-win.

NAMED_LEVEL_SECOND_TEST.pattern_verdict: RETIRE-WITH-EVIDENCE — neither hypothesis flips this pattern to real-fills-positive AND edge_capture-positive AND DSR!=FAIL. baseline-long real-fills exp=$-21.68 (n=361), edge_capture=$-984.6 [NEGATIVE]; Hyp A regime-gate: exp=$-21.19 (n=358, suppressed 4 hostile-regime longs), edge_capture=$-888.6 [NEGATIVE]; Hyp B short-inversion: exp=$-25.38 (n=155), edge_capture=$-10.2 [NEGATIVE]. Per playbook 'setups that fail thresholds get retired, not loosened' + OP-22, this is a clean honest no-win.

op20_disclosures.authority: Real-fills (lib.simulator_real + OPRA options cache, valid through 2026-05-29). BS-sim is ranking-only and is NOT used (C1).
op20_disclosures.level_source_caveat: All 3 watchers read key-levels.json from disk; this run reuses the exit-sweep's monkeypatch forcing each per-day cache to SYNTHETIC PDH/PDL/PDC/PDO proxies (★★/★), NOT production ★★★ named levels (no historical archive). Absolute WR is a proxy LOWER-BOUND. The SIGN questions (does the gate flip edge_capture positive? are the shorts WITH J?) are answerable on the proxy because the level set is identical across all arms.
op20_disclosures.regime_gate_vix_caveat: The Hyp-A regime gate needs vix_now AND vix_prior at the signal bar. The watcher signal metadata carries vix_now; vix_prior is taken from metadata when present and falls back to vix_now otherwise (a CONSERVATIVE fallback — when vix_prior is unavailable the 'VIX rising' clause cannot fire, so the gate suppresses FEWER longs, never more). The dominant hostile clauses (BEAR ribbon + below-VWAP) are computed exactly from rth/ribbon_df at the signal index. Report suppressed_by_regime to see how many longs the gate actually removed.
op20_disclosures.anchor_gate: OP-16 shape on real-fills: edge_capture = sum(pnl on WIN-anchor days) - sum(max(0,-pnl) on LOSS-anchor days). POSITIVE = fires WITH J (makes money on his WIN days, doesn't bleed on his LOSS days). J has 3 WIN (4/29,5/01,5/04) + 3 LOSS (5/05,5/06,5/07) anchor days; these watchers fire on only a subset, so anchor n is tiny — a clean sign here is necessary, not sufficient (C24).
op20_disclosures.dsr_returns: DSR/PSR on per-trade dollar P&L (constant qty=3 notional). PBO skipped (no CSCV per-slice matrix). Advisory only (gate.py is not a hard gate); n_trials=3 (baseline / regime-gated / short arms).
op20_disclosures.premium_stop: premium_stop_pct=-0.99 (disabled) for ALL arms — chart/ribbon/time exits only (C1/C2). Exit geometry held at production; the exit-sweep already proved no exit config rescues these (watcher-exit-sweep.json).
op20_disclosures.not_a_promotion: RESEARCH ONLY. No watcher code, params, or doctrine changed. Watchers remain WATCH_ONLY. PROMOTE-CANDIDATE = 'worth a real-fills re-test on production ★★★ levels'; RETIRE-WITH-EVIDENCE = 'stop engineering this bounce pattern'.

overall_verdict.summary: 0/3 patterns rescued to PROMOTE-CANDIDATE. RETIRE-WITH-EVIDENCE (neither regime-gate nor short-inversion clears the real-fills-positive + with-J + DSR gate): FLOOR_HOLD_BOUNCE, CLOSE_CEILING_FADE, NAMED_LEVEL_SECOND_TEST. Watchers remain WATCH_ONLY regardless; a PROMOTE here only means 'worth a real-fills re-test on production ★★★ levels', a RETIRE means 'stop spending engineering on this bounce pattern' (closes the watcher-fleet question).
